title: HumanUniqueness nullifier is constant across sessions, enabling cross-verifier linkability

artifacts.description: Change public nullifierHash output to Poseidon3(scope, secret, sessionNonce) for per-session unlinkability. Add new private signal externalNullifier = Poseidon2(scope, secret) passed only to an on-chain commitment hash, not exposed as a public output.
New helper circuit (or inline in HumanUniqueness) that proves knowledge of externalNullifier without revealing it — outputs a Poseidon commitment used by the registry for sybil/revocation gating.

Update verifyAndRegister to accept the new public outputs: per-session nullifierHash (used for replay prevention) and externalNullifierCommitment (used for sybil/revocation). Store commitment in mapping, not raw nullifier.
Update interface to reflect the new proof public signals: humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, nonceBinding, externalNullifierCommitment.
Update proveHandshake and verifyHandshake to pass sessionNonce into the circuit witness for nullifierHash derivation. Expose externalNullifierCommitment in the proof output type for registry calls.
Add externalNullifierCommitment to HumanProof type. Deprecate the old flat nullifierHash field in a backwards-compatible way for the transition.

Document the two-nullifier architecture: per-session nullifierHash for replay prevention (visible to verifier) vs externalNullifierCommitment for sybil gating (committed on-chain only). Add privacy threat model section covering cross-verifier linkability.

Add test: same (scope, secret) with different sessionNonce values produces distinct nullifierHash values. Add test: externalNullifierCommitment is stable across sessions for the same identity. Add linkability regression test asserting no two sessions share nullifierHash.

Update integration tests for new public signal layout. Add test: two proofs from the same identity with different nonces both accepted by registry (replay guard uses session nullifier, sybil guard uses commitment).
New doc explaining the two-layer nullifier design, the cross-session linkability threat, and why the fix breaks linkability without sacrificing sybil resistance. Include before/after signal diagrams.

steps: Audit current HumanUniqueness.circom: document existing signals, constraints, and public outputs to establish a baseline before changes.

Design the two-nullifier signal layout on paper: (1) sessionNullifier = Poseidon3(scope, secret, sessionNonce) as the public nullifierHash output, (2) externalNullifier = Poseidon2(scope, secret) as a private intermediate, (3) externalNullifierCommitment = Poseidon1(externalNullifier) as a new public output for on-chain registry.

Update HumanUniqueness.circom: add sessionNonce as a private input signal, replace the Poseidon2(scope, secret) nullifier computation with Poseidon3(scope, secret, sessionNonce), add the externalNullifierCommitment signal, and update the public output declaration.

Re-run trusted setup: since public outputs changed, the existing .zkey is invalid. Generate a new project-specific Groth16 key against pot16.ptau and regenerate the vkey.json. Do NOT use the Semaphore v4 ceremony for this — HumanUniqueness no longer shares the same constraint shape.
Regenerate Solidity verifier from the new vkey.json using snarkjs exportSolidityVerifier. Replace contracts/src/generated/HumanUniquenessVerifier.sol.

Update HumanRegistry.sol: change the verifyAndRegister function signature to accept externalNullifierCommitment as a separate parameter. Use the session nullifierHash for per-session replay prevention (revert if seen in sessionNullifiers mapping). Use externalNullifierCommitment for sybil/revocation gating (revert if commitment is revoked).
Update sdk/src/types.ts: add externalNullifierCommitment to HumanProof. Mark old nullifierHash as the session-scoped value (not the sybil key) in JSDoc.

Update sdk/src/human.ts: pass sessionNonce into the circuit witness builder. Surface externalNullifierCommitment from proof publicSignals. Ensure verifyHandshake validates both the session nullifier (replay) and optionally the commitment (revocation check).
Write circuit tests covering: (a) distinct sessionNonce → distinct nullifierHash, (b) same identity → same externalNullifierCommitment across sessions, (c) invalid witness (wrong secret) rejected.

Write contract integration tests covering: (a) first handshake accepted, (b) replay of same session nullifier rejected, (c) second handshake with new nonce accepted for same identity, (d) revoked commitment rejected.

Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md with the new signal table, a privacy threat model subsection, and a note that verifiers MUST NOT log externalNullifierCommitment for linkability prevention.
Write docs/privacy-nullifier-design.md with before/after signal diagrams and the threat model rationale.
Run full test suite (npm run test:circuits:fast, npm run test:contracts) and fix any failures.
Run npm run test:circuits:slow (full Groth16 proof) to validate the new zkey end-to-end.
Update CHANGELOG and bump SDK minor version (breaking change to HumanProof type and circuit public outputs).
dependencies: circomlib Poseidon3 (3-input hash) — verify it is available in the imported circomlib version
pot16.ptau — must have capacity for ~2200 constraints; current pot16 supports 2^16, sufficient
snarkjs exportSolidityVerifier — required to regenerate verifier contract from new vkey
Hardhat — contract recompile and test re-run after verifier replacement
Semaphore v4 ceremony no longer usable for HumanUniqueness after this change — project must maintain its own Groth16 ceremony artifact
